records.specific_details: The Wheel hosted launch event for our Pre-Budget Submission 2026. It comprised a panel discussion with speakers from community and voluntary organisations and an overview of The Wheel's priorities for Budget 2026. We invited Ministers, TDs and Senators from the most relevant departments and joint Oireachtas committees and several attended in person.
records.intended_results: To inform TDs and Senators about The Wheel's priorities for Budget 2026, which includes issues such as sustainable funding for the sector, equality of pay, increased compliance requirements, and the hidden infrastructure provided by the sector in areas such as health, social care, housing and climate action.

records.specific_details: Email concerning the E-liquids Products Tax and concerns around the mechanism chosen to implement the tax via self-declaration rather than as a tax-stamp. Hale also referred to research commissioned from KPMG which raised several important policy considerations around the E-Liquids products tax including the potential for a surge in illicit and grey market sales.
records.intended_results: A review of the proposal to apply an E-liquids tax using self-declaration mechanism rather than a tax stamp.

records.specific_details: Support for Classification of HHC as a Schedule 1 Controlled Drug
records.intended_results: Offer full support from Hale Vaping, Ireland’s largest indigenous vaping company for the proposed Classification of HHC as a Schedule 1 Controlled Drug by the Government. Hale Vaping is not affiliated with the tobacco industry and has always upheld high standards of compliance and public health responsibility. As such, Hale have never stocked HHC products, which Hale view as unregulated and potentially harmful substance.

dpo_entries.public_body: Dáil Éireann, the Lower House of the Oireachtas
Dáil Éireann, the Lower House of the Oireachtas

lobbying_activities: Offer full support from Hale Vaping, Ireland’s largest indigenous vaping company for the proposed Classification of HHC as a Schedule 1 Controlled Drug by the Government. Hale expressed strong support for the proposed legislative action to classify hexahydrocannabinol (HHC) as a Schedule 1 controlled drug under the Misuse of Drugs Act 1977. Hale Vaping is not affiliated with the tobacco industry and has always upheld high standards of compliance and public health responsibility. - Email

records.specific_details: AV room launch of paper on Irelands role in the fossil fuel industry where we shared the findings and recommendations.
records.intended_results: For DPOs to be made aware of our findings and push for recommendations on regulating private finance going through Ireland to the fossil fuel industry. Which found that as of June 2024, Irish-based subsidiaries of investment companies held €31.76 billion ($34 billion) in fossil fuel investments. This puts Ireland 14th globally in terms of fossil fuel investment by manager location, alongside economies that have substantial fossil fuel industries.

records.lobbyist_name: Reduced Risk Products Association Company Limited
records.specific_details: The Reduced Risk Products Association conducted analysis of Census 2022 and the results of the Healthy Ireland Survey 2024 to examine the likely implications of proposed restrictions on flavoured vapes for adult vapers including smokers who rely on vapes to quit or reduce their smoking returning to smoking, with consequent impacts on deaths from smoking. This analysis was prepared on a Dail constituency basis and sent to all members of Dáil Eireann.
records.intended_results: To inform public representatives of the implications of proposed restrictions on flavoured vapes for adult vapers and for the health outlook of smokers who use vaping to quit or reduce their levels of smoking.

records.specific_details: Support Needed for Tipperary Businesses After Severe Snow
records.intended_results: Support Needed for Tipperary Businesses After Severe Snow
lobbying_activities: 85% of businesses had to close, with some shut for several days.Many lost between €1,000–€5,000, while others lost even more.57% faced extra costs for heating, repairs, or other issues.Almost 25% think it will take over a month to recover fully.Grants to help with their losses and extra costs.Relief on commercial rates to ease their financial strain.Support for energy costs to deal with high heating bills.Better snow and ice management in commercial areas to avoid future disruption. - Meeting

records.intended_results: To raise awareness of Oireachtas members on the urgent need for extra time for students with dyslexia and other learning difficulties when sitting State Exams (Junior Cycle and Leaving Certificate) as part of the accommodations offered within the RACE (Reasonable Accommodations at the Certificate Examinations) scheme as administered by the State Examinations Commission (SEC).

records.specific_details: Oireachtas Presentation for Dyslexia Awareness Month presenting findings from a recent survey on the experiences of Irish people with dyslexia, and highlighting issues which need to be addressed.
records.intended_results: To build awareness of Oireachtas members on the lived experience of dyslexia and the need for: (1) teacher training on dyslexia (2) equitable access to dyslexia assessment (3) access to quality evidence-based supports in school for students with dyslexia, (4) formal recognition of the right to a dyslexic identity, with timely access to assessment and appropriate supports and (5)  need to move to evidence-of-need approach to allocation of accommodations (instead of ‘evidence-of-deficit’ approach)

records.specific_details: Oireachtas Presentation for Dyslexia Awareness Month presenting findings from a recent survey on the experiences of Irish people with dyslexia, and highlighting issues which need to be addressed.
records.intended_results: To build awareness of Oireachtas members on the lived experience of dyslexia and the need for: (1) accurate, timely identification and support which can be equitably accessed (2) a more developed continuum of support for students with dyslexia (3) mandatory training of all teachers on dyslexia (4) improved use of technology in school and in exams (5) senior cycle reform and the risk to mental health and wellbeing is change doesn’t happen.
lobbying_activities: Invitations to Oireachas members before the event, and distribution of the PDF version of the Oireachtas briefing to Oireachtas members (after the event). - Email

records.specific_details: asthma health policies and programmes, Access to medication, access to healthcare providers, access to diagnostics, expansion of existing programmes, air quality measures
records.intended_results: To establish support for these policies among election candidates
lobbying_activities: Phone calls to offices of TDs previously supportive of the organisation - Phone call

lobbying_activities: Email

records.specific_details: 8th January 2020 Dissemination of Brexit Policy Paper - How to Make Brexit Work for All Part 2: Signposts for Services
records.intended_results: To promote a strong post-Brexit UK-EU relationship and trade deal.
lobbying_activities: Mailout sent to all TDs providing overview of the proposal and inviting them to engage with the Chamber - Email

records.specific_details: Increase budget of Social Inclusion Community Activation Programme
records.intended_results: Seeking commitment to restore the Social inclusion Community Activation Program funding from national level of €38m to the 2008 level of €84.7m
lobbying_activities: Letter to all candidates in the Tipperary Constituency general election Feb 2020 - Letter

records.specific_details: Policy changes on TUS and RSS programmes
records.intended_results: Individuals on Disability Benefit participate on Tus::Participants over the age 63 to stay on Tus till retirement age, rather than having to leave after one year and face the prospect of never egtting work in the future::Young people under 25 remain on Tus for a minimum of 2 years::Reduce the bureaucracy associated with applying for the RSS scheme::Expanding the criteria for eligibility for the RSS scheme::Streamlining the means assessment process for RSS::Restore the Rural Social Scheme to the Department of Agriculture
lobbying_activities: Letter forwarded to all candidates in Tipperary constituency  election Feb 2020 - Letter

records.specific_details: Simplify and Protect Leader Program
records.intended_results: A simplified less bureaucratic and Rural Development Program::Approval of Leader projects should revert to Local Development Companies for the new leader programme in keeping with the bottom up ethos of leader::Protect 7 core Leader Principles::Pursue extra EU funding opportunities through the community led local development approach::Negotiation of new CAP should include increased role for Leader::Ensure a seamless transfer for the current Leader program to the new Leader program::Increase Exchequer funding to the Leader programme to offset the expected reduction in the EU contribution to Leader::Apply to the EU for exemption from State Aid (De Minimis) rules for leader programme similar to many other funding programmes::Restore the ability of the Leader Programme to animate communities and projects
lobbying_activities: Letter to all Tipperary General Election Candidates - Letter

records.specific_details: CWI issued a submission developed jointly by a range of NGOs on the impact of COVID-19 on the most vulnerable groups and a planning framework to mitigate against the worst impacts
records.intended_results: To provide evidence and support planning to mitigate against the worst effects of COVID-19 on already vulnerable and marginalized groups and communities

dpo_entries.public_body: Department of Rural and Community Development
Department of Rural and Community Development
Department of Children and Youth Affairs
Department of Public Expenditure and Reform

lobbying_activities: Both the submission and the planning framework were circulated to TDs, Senators, government departments and the NEPHT CoVID-19 Vulnerable Groups subcommittee - Email
One follow-up virtual meeting was held with a TD - Virtual Meeting
